apsNote: Health Sciences closes 31 July. Applications processed on rolling basis.
requiredDocuments: Certified copy of South African ID or valid passport
Certified copy of matric certificate or statement of results
Proof of residence (not older than 3 months)

internationalStudents.note: International applicants require SAQA evaluation of qualifications.

apsSystem: NSC points /42 (top 6 subjects, excluding Life Orientation)

flags: applicationOpens: 1 March 2026 is widely reported by multiple sources but not explicitly stated in the official Study Opportunities 2027 PDF (which only states closing is 30 September 2026); treat as high-confidence but not page-confirmed
healthSciencesCloses: no earlier Health Sciences-specific closing date found in the official Study Opportunities 2027 PDF; all programmes listed with 30 September 2026 closing date
Most Diploma programme-level APS thresholds not explicitly published as a single number in the official PDF — entry requirements specify subject ratings rather than a total APS; aps:0 set for those
Engineering degree entry: ranked by sum of Mathematics and Physical Science scores rather than a single APS minimum; 28 points is the general NSC degree entry threshold stated
BHSc Chiropractic: listed as 30 points — sourced from third-party compilation; not independently confirmed from official Study Opportunities 2027 PDF page
prospectusUrl: PDF at this path confirmed to exist and downloaded (4.7MB, published May 2026) but the URL may change — verify at dut.ac.za/apply-4/
